content: I am interested in using rust language for linux kernel, so I would like to know what direction we plan to work on rust-next. Thanks
What information are you looking for? Do you have a specific driver you want to work on?
Well, I just want to know what type of commit I can make if I want to commit patch to linux/rust, or there is no limit on what type of commit to receive.
In general, patches sent to the mailing list should either be new drivers, or dependencies of new drivers.
How can we build infrastructure for Rust in the kernel if we want to do so?

wilson wang said:

Well, I just want to know what type of commit I can make if I want to commit patch to linux/rust, or there is no limit on what type of commit to receive.

What Alice said, plus contributing Rust to Linux kernel is similar to contributing C code to Linux kernel. In my personal experience, it will help if you begin to review areas you are interested in, you may find ideas or things to improve during your review. Besides, it may not be a bad idea to review and contribute C code as a starter, which could give you more visibility in the community and earn the trust.
